title: forge-runtime
description: Machine-readable API manifest for AI agent tool discovery. Describes the public API surface of the forge-runtime package.

forge.config.description: Typed, validated, layered configuration from env vars, .env, and TOML.

functions.name: require
functions.description: Validate that required config keys are present at startup. Raises ConfigurationError if any are missing.

functions.name: override
functions.signature: override(values: dict[str, Any]) -> Generator[None, None, None]
functions.description: Context manager for overriding config values in tests.

forge.log.description: Structured JSON logging with automatic trace context propagation.

functions.name: get
functions.description: Get a logger instance for the given module name.

functions.name: context
functions.description: Context manager for binding key-value pairs to all log entries within the context.

forge.retry.description: Retry logic with exponential backoff, jitter, and circuit breaker.

classes.name: CircuitBreaker
classes.description: Circuit breaker that prevents retries after a configurable failure threshold.

functions.name: retry
functions.signature: retry(attempts: int = 3, backoff: str = 'exponential', exceptions: list[type[Exception]] | None = None, circuit_breaker: CircuitBreaker | None = None)
functions.description: Decorator for async functions that retries on failure with configurable backoff.

functions.name: attempt
functions.description: Async context manager for manual retry loops.

forge.ai.description: Unified AI model interface across OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Ollama.

classes.name: Message
classes.description: A chat message with role (system, user, assistant) and content.

classes.name: AIResponse
classes.signature: AIResponse(content: str, model: str, usage: dict[str, int])
classes.description: Response from an AI model call including content, model used, and token usage.

functions.name: complete
functions.signature: complete(messages: list[Message], model: str | None = None, max_tokens: int | None = None, output_schema: type[BaseModel] | None = None, fallback_models: list[str] | None = None) -> AIResponse
functions.description: Send a completion request to an AI model. Supports structured output via Pydantic schemas with automatic retry on invalid output.

functions.name: stream
functions.signature: stream(messages: list[Message], model: str | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> AsyncIterator[StreamChunk]
functions.description: Stream a completion response from an AI model. Yields content delta chunks.
functions.example: async for chunk in stream(messages=[Message.user('Tell me a story')]): ...

forge.health.description: Kubernetes-compatible /health and /ready endpoints with custom check registration.

functions.name: check
functions.description: Decorator for registering a custom health check function.

forge.cache.description: Decorator-based caching with in-memory LRU and Redis backends.

functions.name: cached
functions.signature: cached(ttl: int = 300, key: str | None = None, backend: str | None = None)
functions.description: Decorator that caches async function results with configurable TTL.

functions.name: invalidate
functions.description: Invalidate a cached entry by key.

forge.validation.description: Pydantic-integrated input validation with consistent error responses.

functions.name: validate
functions.description: Decorator that validates function arguments against type annotations using Pydantic.

forge.core.description: Core runtime with module registry, DI container, event bus, and lifecycle management.

classes.name: ForgeModule
classes.description: Base class for all forge modules. Subclasses must implement the 'name' property.

classes.name: Runtime
classes.description: Central runtime coordinator that manages module lifecycle, DI, and events.
